title: 1.2: States that kitty can be launched as a single isolated instance using flags such as --single-instance and --instance-group
1.3: States that the choose-fonts kitten can be invoked using syntax like kitty +kitten choose-fonts or kitten choose-fonts
1.4: States that choose-fonts subcommand is registered via EntryPoint function using root.AddSubCommand with an Options struct containing Reload_in
1.5: States that the --reload-in option accepts choices such as parent, all, and none, with parent as the default
1.6: States that option values are parsed and passed to the main function (e.g., via GetOptionValues populating an Options struct)
1.7: States that pressing Enter at the final step triggers config patching via event.MatchesPressOrRepeat("enter")
1.8: States that config patching updates font settings such as font_family, bold_font, italic_font, and bold_italic_font within a marked section (e.g., KITTY_FONTS sentinel)
1.9: States that config reload behavior depends on the reload option value (e.g., parent reloads only the parent instance, all reloads all instances, none skips reload)
1.10: States that a runtime test demonstrates kitty.conf is created with the selected font settings in a marked section (e.g., # BEGIN_KITTY_FONTS ... font_family ... # END_KITTY_FONTS)
1.12: States that a fresh kitty instance started with the same config directory loads the persisted font configuration, confirming fonts persist across restarts
2.1: Claims that choose-fonts modifies kitty source code files or internal binaries instead of kitty.conf
